title: Playwright Rendering, Isolation, Readiness, and Dynamic-DOM Evidence · Type-System Trace: Readiness Contract
summary: Every page launches a browser, shares cookies and storage, waits for network idle, captures unstable DOM, and leaves contexts or downloads alive. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts readiness contract increment in isolated evidence-driven rendering adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, c…
objectives: Wait for application-specific stable evidence under time and request budgets instead of treating network idle as universal readiness.
Create bounded incognito-like BrowserContexts with explicit storage, permission, download, service-worker, locale, viewport, and close policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Predict Readiness Contract · Theory Readiness Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 2036 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove necessity, context isolation, readiness, browser network policy, provenance, and closure; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Build Readiness Contract evidence · Theory Readiness Contra…
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-readiness-contract-1v9qy6" that uses case 8195 to prove necessity, context isolation, readiness, browser network policy, provenance, and closure. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Readiness Contract model · Theory Readiness Cont…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: networkidle means every dynamic application is complete.
